title: Add a --workdir option to 'podman exec'
body:
This is one way to [retain the current working directory](https://github.com/debarshiray/fedora-toolbox/issues/20) while entering the Silverblue toolbox. The Silverblue toolbox container has the `$HOME` bind mounted from the host, so in a lot of cases the current working directory on the host would be a valid path inside the container.

It is similar to what `gnome-terminal` does to retain the current working directory for shells. It uses `chdir` right after forking the child process before using `exec` to start the shell.

The other option that I briefly considered was:
```
podman exec ... container "cd /some/path && /bin/bash"
```
However, that doesn't work because `cd /some/path && /bin/bash` isn't a valid `argv` in itself.

I am currently unsure about the error handling. I noticed that `podman run` actually creates the working directory if it isn't present. Is that also what `podman exec` should do? Or should it error out, as is the case currently?

Creating an absent or invalid working directory wouldn't be what the Silverblue toolbox would do, though, which complicates things a bit. I could use `cd /some/path` in a separate `podman exec` invocation to check the path, but that would be prone to races. :/

comments.body: @rhatdan @baude If there are n objections I think we ought to tag this as milestone 1.0

Sorry about that. I thought that the easiest way to run the test suite would be to rely on the CI, so the PR got created with that typo.

Fixed.
